title: Knema stylosa
taxonomy: Knema stylosa is classified as a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as nutmegs or myristicas. The Myristicaceae family consists of approximately 180 species spread across 100 genera. Knema stylosa is a part of the genus Knema, which comprises about 60 species of flowering plants. The classification of Knema stylosa is part of the ongoing efforts in botanical research to categorize and understand the diversity of plant species.

intro: Knema stylosa, also known as the Stylosa tree, is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Knema stylosa include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Knema stylosa is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, with the most common preparations being a leaf decoction used to treat fever and a root decoction used to treat respiratory issues.
